summary:
The placement of an indirect retainer should closely follow the direct retainer to maintain structural integrity and support during treatment. This ensures optimal force management around restored teeth areas.
The decision was made based on ensuring adequate force control through strategic positioning relative to other retention elements used clinically. \boxed{\text{Near}} To summarize Agent2’s reasoning into two sentences focusing only on critical aspects related to placing an indirect retainer:

An indirect retainer must be located nearby its corresponding direct retainer to effectively manage forces and stabilize tooth position; thus, it will generally be situated adjacent or very proximal to the primary retaining element. Placing it this way supports overall oral health by enhancing mechanical efficiency and reducing potential complications associated with improper spacing between these components. 

Final boxed summary sentence provided as requested:\(\boxed{\text{near}}\)
